body.presenting_problem: Fourth CBT session for panic disorder. Client continues thought journal work, successfully interrupted a panic cycle in a grocery store using cognitive techniques learned in therapy.
mood_indicators: Panic attack frequency decreased from daily to 3-4 per week
Client described feeling 'empowered' after interrupting panic cycle
Terror intensity rated 9/10 during highway panic, reduced later
Belief in alternative thoughts: 30% in-moment, 80% post-event
key_themes: Cognitive restructuring of catastrophic health anxiety
Gap between in-moment fear and post-event rational evaluation
Emerging self-efficacy in managing panic symptoms
therapeutic_interventions: Cognitive restructuring (automatic thought identification and challenging)
body.client_progress: Meaningful improvement in 4 sessions. Panic frequency reduced from daily to 3-4/week. Client successfully used cognitive restructuring mid-panic for the first time (grocery store incident). Avoidance behavior (highway) persists and will be addressed next via graded exposure.
homework_assigned: Continue thought journal with added element: rate believability of alternative thought at time of event and again one hour later
Track panic frequency for the week
recommended_followup: Begin exposure hierarchy for driving situations next session
Start with low-anxiety driving exposures (parking lot, short local drives)
Reassess GAD-7 at session 6 to measure progress

similar_sessions.relevant_excerpt: Follow-up session for panic disorder. Client has been practicing interoceptive exposure exercises as homework. Reports successfully completing 3 of 5 assigned exposures. Panic frequency decreased from 4/week to 1/week. GAD-7 improved from 18 to 11.
similar_sessions.relevance_reason: Later session for the same client showing continued progress trajectory — from 3-4 attacks/week (session 4) to 1/week (session 12), validating the CBT approach.
